Advanced Eye Treatment in Kochi – Keratoconus Clinic

The Keratoconus Clinic at Giridhar Eye Institute, located in Kerala, is dedicated to the diagnosis and treatment of keratoconus, a progressive eye disorder where the normally round cornea thins and begins to bulge into a cone-like shape. This condition can cause distorted vision and, if left untreated, may lead to significant visual impairment. Our clinic is equipped with advanced diagnostic tools and offers a range of treatment options to manage this condition effectively. We understand that early detection and appropriate intervention are crucial to preserving vision and improving the quality of life for those affected by keratoconus.

Precision Diagnostics

Early keratoconus diagnosis with cutting-edge technology, ensuring timely intervention.

Personalized Treatment Plans

Customized treatment plans with cross-linking, lenses, or surgery.

Access to the Latest Treatments

Up-to-date treatments and advanced surgical techniques for keratoconus.

Expert Specialists Patient Education

Expert care and education for informed eye health decisions.

Your Path to Expert Keratoconus Care

Choosing the right clinic for keratoconus treatment is crucial for managing this progressive condition effectively. At Giridhar Eye Institute, we are committed to providing world-class care with a compassionate touch. Our clinic in Kerala is recognized for its patient-centered approach, where each individual is treated with the utmost care and respect. We combine our deep understanding of local healthcare needs with international standards of excellence to deliver the best outcomes for our patients. Whether you are newly diagnosed or seeking a second opinion, our Keratoconus Clinic is here to guide you through every step of your treatment journey, ensuring the best possible vision outcomes.

Frequently Asked Questions

Get quick answers to common questions about keratoconus, its management, and treatment options to help you navigate your eye care journey.

Can keratoconus cause blindness?

Keratoconus doesn’t cause complete blindness but can severely impact vision if untreated. Proper management preserves vision.

Will I need a corneal transplant?

Corneal transplants are only needed in advanced cases. Many patients benefit from glasses, contact lenses, or cross-linking.

Can keratoconus be cured?

Keratoconus cannot be cured, but treatments like cross-linking and lenses can slow progression and improve vision.

How do I know if I need cross-linking?

Your eye specialist will recommend cross-linking based on the progression and severity of your keratoconus.

Is keratoconus hereditary?

Keratoconus can have a genetic link, meaning it may run in families, but it doesn’t always.

Can I wear contact lenses with keratoconus?

Yes, specially designed lenses like scleral or gas-permeable lenses are often used to manage keratoconus.

 

How often do I need checkups for keratoconus?

Regular checkups, usually every 6-12 months, help monitor disease progression and adjust treatment if needed.

Will keratoconus affect both eyes?

Keratoconus often affects both eyes, though severity may differ. Treatment plans are tailored for each eye’s condition.
